name: StreamYard Live Streaming API
what_it_does: Browser-based live streaming and recording studio — enabling creators and businesses to simultaneously broadcast to multiple platforms (YouTube, Facebook, LinkedIn, Twitch) with guest management, custom branding overlays, screen sharing, and recording without software installation.
use_cases: Automating StreamYard broadcast session creation for scheduled live events via API
Syncing live stream metadata and VOD recordings to content management systems
Integrating StreamYard guest invitation workflow with event management platforms
Pulling broadcast performance metrics for social media analytics dashboards
Triggering post-stream workflows (upload, transcript, distribution) via webhook
not_for: High-quality podcast recording (use Riverside.fm for separate track recording)
Video conferencing and team meetings (use Zoom or Teams)
Professional broadcast production requiring hardware encoders
best_when: A creator, marketer, or media team needs to simultaneously broadcast live to multiple social platforms with guest management and custom branding, without installing software.
avoid_when: You need separate track recording for podcast production, local recording for quality, or professional broadcast hardware integration.

auth.notes: OAuth2 authorization code flow. Scopes for broadcast read/write and destination management. Acquired by Hopin (2021) then returned to independent operation. API primarily available to platform partners and integrators. No webhooks documented.

pricing.notes: Founded 2018. Acquired by Hopin 2021 for $250M; later became independent again. Very popular with creators and small media teams. 6+ simultaneous destination streaming. Custom overlays and branding. Primarily a product rather than platform — API access is limited.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Limited public API — primarily partner-facing OAuth2 integration. OAuth2 scopes for broadcast management. No webhook documentation found. Primary interaction is through UI. API coverage is partial — many product features not exposed via API. Avoid for complex automation workflows.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Broadcast reads idempotent. Destination reads idempotent. Broadcast creation may not be idempotent — verify before retry.
known_agent_gotchas: API is primarily for platform partners — consumer developers have limited access
No webhooks — polling required to detect broadcast completion
Multistream destinations must be connected in UI before API management
RTMP destinations configured per broadcast — not reusable across sessions by default
VOD recording availability after live stream has processing delay

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. US data residency. OAuth2 with scopes. Live streaming platform — RTMP stream keys are sensitive credentials. Guest invite links are time-limited. RTMP ingest keys must be secured.
